reviews.review_msg: Prof. Kropinski is excellent. She is very caring and when you will look back you will understand how well the course was taught. She went out of her way to show us a neat technique of dimensional analysis (which is not required to be taught), which has stuck with me ever since.
The best professor I had at SFU. Assignments, midterm and final were very fair. Don't be intimidated if she seems cold, because she is an amazing, caring person and has a great sense of humor.
Fluid Dynamics is a difficult course. She made did an excellent job presenting the material. Her exams were completely fair, and her grading was generous.
Exams are fair and quite similar to the previous ones. Lectures are informative and amazing. She's there to help you if you come to her office hours. Would take other courses with her.
Lecture are quite dry, the teaching skill was ...  never never smile, always keep a straight face

She cares her students and tries to increase your grades. She always prepares sample test and exercises for us. However, she teaches not quite clear. I think TA taught better than her. The course material is also very hard, so you must work hard and go to ask TA when you have problems.

This course is very straight forward and almost entirely computational. If you are interested in E&M or fluids, or things you learned about in calc 3, this is a good starting point. Kropinski is a great teacher in my opinion, no chip on her shoulder, a sense of humour and a very reasonable MT and final. The assignments were long and grueling though

lectures may be boring and monotonic. but it is worth to go to class because she would give some hint on midterm questions. The midterm was not hard but it is based on concept and too long. at last she allowed a double-sided cheatsheet in final exam which reduces the memorization part. the questions stay very close to the practice final. recommend.

First impression is she is very severe, but that wears off after a couple of lectures actually quite friendly.  Final is based on the last two weeks of class and was moderately difficult, all exams are fair, but homework can be quite time consuming especially the questions that she adds.

This class was pretty slow up until the end, and the final focused primarily on the material in the last few weeks. She may seem scary but she's very approachable. I recommend this course if you liked 251 and wanted to know why some of the methods in that course worked.

She didn't seem very interested in the course but she taught really well and clear. The pace was kind of slow throughout the semester and it seems like all final exam stuff happened in the last weeks weeks.

She teaches the material at a suitable pace for people who are new to the topic. Examples are insightful and she explains them well. I think people would have a better impression of her if she acts a little happier about what she's doing. (I understand it's hard sometimes, but just a comment)

It's not her fault the material is so darn hard..As a prof, she explained the material well, you just need to FOCUS and CONCENTRATE and be very very good in math.. It's really nice to her to make the final so easy so that everyone can pass. Also, she ended up scaling the whole thing.. so just hope you're smarter than the person next to you

The exams were quite hard. The final was the easiest of the bunch, but was still challenging. The midterms were too long to be finished in the alloted time frame. Was against scaling at first because she has 'taught the class enough to tune it quite well'. Scaled in the end. She teaches as if it is your only course.

Did a fairly good job of teaching a difficult class.  This course has a very high volume of material to cover, and Dr. Kropinksi kept a fairly rapid pace.  As expected for this level of math, expects you to do a very large amount of work. She does, however, seem to have very high expectations, which is both good and bad.
seemed like she was trying to fail 90% of the class.

She is a very good lecturer, as she goes through material in constant rate giving you all you need to know for homework and exams. She is clear and concise, which is very important in Math classes. Love her loud voice: no need for a microphone at all. Pretty good organization of notes and clear writing.
Bad, bad professor. I wonder how she got her doctoral degree.. she makes a billion mistakes in her ill-prepared lectures.
She's an OK lecturer, not the most interesting but not too bad either. Her voice is definitely boring but she does crack a joke on occasion. I think her exams were FAIR, though the averages were low.

My impression of Prof Kropinski:  "Oh darn, I've made my third mistake in lecture today.  Let's all take a five minute break while I sort through my notes up at the front here."  I'm sure if you're a patient person or are REALLY interested in the material she's trying to teach you'll be okay.  Otherwise, avoid this prof or learn from the text.

She does have a boring voice, but if you really pay attention during lecture, everything will be fine.
 Midterm exams weren't too difficult but watch out on the final, it's a bit trickier.
I actually really liked this math class.  I've had trouble with math in the past and I was able to get an A in her class so she must be doing something right!
she's a good lecturer.
A really bad prof. Her letures are totally confusing. She seems like never preparing for her lectures.

I found her lectures entirely useless. i would go to her class and not understand a single thing she was teaching, then i would go off and study the material in the text and it would make sense in moments. she makes easy topics and material difficult!

very unclear notes, monotone..her notes are useless because she does everything the harder way and explains things u dont need to know like why the quadratic eqn is the way it is..for math 100? we dont need to know that!

Very confusing lecture. Have no clue how to copy her notes. Unprepare as well, sometimes doesn't bring overhead pens, have to copy from the blackboard. Midterms and Finals are HARD. Use differnet variables in each lecture for the same equation! LOST...
Final=disaster. Super Long + gets Super Messy(since no calculator is allowed). She's willing to help alot but i see no one attends her OffHour. well.. Other than that, she's a good prof!

Doesn't seem like she knows what she is doing unless she has prepared in advance (ie.  Can't really ask questions in class and expect them answered)  Exams were easier than my previous prof for this course.
Notes are too hard to understand.
Monotonous voice, useless notes.
